skill.name: lottie
skill.description: Adapter patterns for embedding Lottie and dotLottie animations in HyperFrames video compositions.
skill.summary: Integrate Lottie animations in HyperFrames using lottie-web and dotLottie player adapters.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 31 static findings are false positives. The 24 external_commands detections match markdown backtick syntax for code blocks and inline code formatting, not Ruby shell execution. The 5 network findings match CDN library URLs in code examples and documentation reference links, which are standard for a library integration skill. The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance alerts are scanner errors on YAML frontmatter and markdown headings. This skill is documentation-only with no executable code, no network calls at runtime, and no security risk.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Characters Misidentified as Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 24 instances of backtick characters as Ruby shell execution. All matches are markdown syntax: code fence delimiters (three backticks) around HTML/CSS/JS code blocks and inline code formatting. No Ruby code or shell execution exists in this file. This is a well-known false positive pattern when scanning markdown files with pattern-based detectors that do not understand markdown syntax.

low_findings.title: CDN Library URLs in Code Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 5 URLs as hardcoded network references. Two are CDN script sources for Lottie libraries (lottie-web from cdnjs and dotLottie from unpkg) inside markdown code examples. Three are documentation reference links (GitHub repository, wiki, and developer docs). These are legitimate citation and dependency references in an integration skill, not network exfiltration or suspicious activity.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Alarms on Metadata and Headings
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm finding on line 3 and system reconnaissance on line 91. Line 3 is YAML frontmatter description text with no cryptographic content. Line 91 is the markdown heading '## Avoid' with no reconnaissance activity. Both are scanner false positives.

content.user_title: Integrate Lottie Animations into HyperFrames Videos
content.value_statement: Lottie animation files do not work directly in HyperFrames video compositions because the player objects need deterministic seeking. This skill provides adapter patterns to register lottie-web and dotLottie players so HyperFrames can render them frame-accurately.
actual_capabilities: Register lottie-web animation instances on window.__hfLottie for seekable playback in HyperFrames
Integrate dotLottie web players into HyperFrames compositions using standard DotLottie APIs
Load local Lottie JSON and .lottie files from the project assets directory for rendering
Configure animations with deterministic settings (no autoplay, no loop) for frame-accurate seeking
Support multiple simultaneous Lottie animations in a single composition via shared registry
Provide code templates for embedding CDN-loaded lottie-web and dotLottie libraries in composition HTML
limitations: Cannot use remote path URLs for Lottie assets at render time; all assets must be local
Some Adobe After Effects effects and expressions may not survive Lottie export and will not render
Asynchronous player loading may cause registration to miss HyperFrames page validation timing
Three-dimensional layers and camera movements in After Effects have limited Lottie support

use_cases.title: Logo Reveals and Brand Animations
use_cases.description: Embed Lottie logo animations into HyperFrames video compositions for brand intros and outros.
use_cases.target_user: Motion designer creating branded video content

use_cases.title: Product UI Motion
use_cases.description: Add Lottie micro-interactions and UI animations to product demo videos.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developer building interactive product showcases

use_cases.title: After Effects Export Integration
use_cases.description: Convert Adobe After Effects compositions to Lottie format and make them render deterministically in HyperFrames.
use_cases.target_user: Video editor transitioning AE motion graphics to code-based video pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Basic lottie-web Integration
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user embedding a simple Lottie logo animation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to embed a Lottie animation file in my HyperFrames video composition. The animation is a logo reveal saved as assets/logo-reveal.json. Show me the complete HTML template using lottie-web loaded from CDN, with autoplay disabled and the animation registered on window.__hfLottie.

prompt_templates.title: dotLottie Player Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: User embedding a .lottie file with the dotLottie web player
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a product animation saved as assets/product-flow.lottie. Create a HyperFrames composition that loads this file using @lottiefiles/dotlottie-web from CDN. Include a canvas element, disable autoplay and loop, and register the player on window.__hfLottie.

prompt_templates.title: Managing Multiple Synchronized Animations
prompt_templates.scenario: User combining several Lottie animations in one composition
prompt_templates.prompt: My HyperFrames composition needs three Lottie animations: a background particle effect, a logo reveal, and a call-to-action icon. Show me how to load all three using lottie-web, register each one on window.__hfLottie, and keep their containers stable with CSS.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Deterministic Seeking
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user optimizing frame-accurate playback behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a complex HyperFrames scene with multiple dotLottie players and need precise frame control. Show me how to configure each player for deterministic seeking, what container CSS ensures stable dimensions, and how to verify all players are registered before HyperFrames validation runs.

output_examples.input: Embed a simple logo reveal animation for a video intro
output_examples.output: You get a complete HTML composition with a Lottie animation container, CDN-loaded lottie-web library, and the animation configured with autoplay off and loop off. The player is registered on window.__hfLottie so HyperFrames can seek through every frame of the logo reveal.

output_examples.input: Set up a product demo with multiple animated UI elements
output_examples.output: The skill generates a composition with three synchronized Lottie layers: a background particle animation, a product highlight animation, and a text reveal. All players are pushed to the shared window.__hfLottie registry and each container has fixed CSS dimensions to prevent layout shifts during seeking.

best_practices: Load all Lottie assets from local project files instead of remote URLs for reliable offline rendering
Always set autoplay to false and loop to false so HyperFrames has full control over the animation timeline
Use fixed CSS width and height on Lottie container elements to prevent layout instability during frame seeking
anti_patterns: Using remote path URLs for Lottie assets at render time, which breaks offline rendering reliability
Calling play() on animations instead of relying on HyperFrames seeking through goToAndStop
Loading players asynchronously without confirming registration timing before HyperFrames validation

faq.question: What is the difference between lottie-web and dotLottie?
faq.answer: lottie-web loads JSON animation files exported from After Effects. dotLottie loads .lottie files which are compressed bundles that can contain multiple animations and assets. Both are supported by this skill.

faq.question: Why must I disable autoplay?
faq.answer: HyperFrames controls the animation timeline by seeking to specific frames for video rendering. If autoplay is enabled, the animation plays immediately and conflicts with HyperFrames frame control.

faq.question: Can I use Lottie animations hosted on a remote server?
faq.answer: No. Remote path URLs are not reliable at render time. Always download Lottie files to your project assets directory and reference them with local paths.

faq.question: How do I add multiple animations to one scene?
faq.answer: Create separate container elements for each animation, load each one with lottie.loadAnimation, and push every player instance to the window.__hfLottie array.

faq.question: What Adobe After Effects features do not export to Lottie?
faq.answer: Expressions, 3D layers, cameras, lights, and some layer effects do not survive Lottie export. Test your exported JSON file in a browser before using it in HyperFrames.

faq.question: Why is my Lottie animation not appearing in the rendered video?
faq.answer: Check that you registered the player on window.__hfLottie, set autoplay to false, and that the container has explicit CSS dimensions. Also verify the animation loads before HyperFrames validation runs.
